channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Bessent to divest from assets on road to leading Treasury
item.description: President-elect Trump’s Treasury Secretary pick Scott Bessent has plans to divest from funds and other investments.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: DeSantis calls special legislative session on illegal immigration
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Major blue city mayor silent after illegal immigrant uses shelter for criminal activity
item.description: Democratic Mayor Michelle Wu is keeping silent after an illegal immigrant was caught using a taxpayer-funded migrant shelter in the Boston suburbs for criminal activity, including storing a weapon and nearly $1 million worth of drugs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth's views on women in combat, infidelity and more
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pete Hegseth will try to convince members of the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday that he should be the next secretary of defense, following weeks of meetings with lawmakers who peppered him with questions about his character and views on a range of military issues.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Incoming Trump team is questioning civil servants at National Security Council about their loyalty
item.description: Incoming senior Trump officials have begun questioning career civil servants serving on the White House National Security Council about who they voted for, their political contributions in the 2024 election and whether they've made social media posts that could be considered incriminating by the Tru
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Major push to protect, renew Trump tax cuts launched by powerful conservative group
item.description: A top fiscally conservative political advocacy group is launching a $20 million campaign to urge Congress "to protect prosperity" by renewing the Trump tax cuts, which expire this year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk, AI and tech titans, venture capitalists invited to pre-inauguration dinner at dawn of Trump era
item.description: Outside the Box Ventures to host a gathering of top tech CEOs and venture capitalists with members of the incoming Trump administration in Washington, D.C., ahead of the inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: West Virginia's conservative shift could sharpen under its incoming governor
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — West Virginia Gov. Jim Justice signed a number of socially conservative laws during his two terms in office. But he rarely pushed for them. The Democrat-turned-Republican, now heading to the U.S. Senate to take over the seat of former Independent U.S. Sen.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A tough-on-crime approach is back in US state capitols
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — Within minutes of his inauguration Monday, new Missouri Gov. Mike Kehoe is expected to issue a variety of orders targeting crime. The tone-setting move reflects a national trend.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian leader meets with Trump at Mar-a-Lago to discusses energy relationship
item.description: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith said she met with President-elect Trump and members of his transition team at Mar-a-Lago and discussed a U.S.-Canadian energy relationship.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance says those who ‘committed violence’ on Jan. 6 ‘obviously’ shouldn’t be pardoned
item.description: (The Hill) -- Vice President-elect JD Vance said people who “committed violence” on Jan. 6 “obviously” shouldn’t be pardoned by President-elect Trump. “I think it’s very simple.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom defends fire response with fact-check site linking to Dem Party fundraising platform
item.description: Democratic California Gov. Gavin Newsom is combating "misinformation" surrounding his handling of the fires with a website that asks for donations through ActBlue.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden awards Pope Francis with highest civilian honor, Presidential Medal of Freedom, over the phone
item.description: President Joe Biden changed his travel plans to Rome to award Pope Francis the Medal of Freedom due to the California wildfires, but the two still spoke on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans urge Trump nominees: Don't take the bait from Democrats
item.description: Senate Republicans are urging President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees to resist taking the bait from Democrats when their high-stakes confirmation hearings kick off next week, marking a make-or-break moment for some of his choices.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Family of American hostage in Gaza remains hopeful their son, 21, will return
item.description: Edan Alexander, an American hostage, celebrated his second birthday in captivity after Hamas kidnapped him more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Hena Doba, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Cabinet picks are set for Senate hearings. Here's the schedule
item.description: After the initial crush of personnel announcements for President-elect Donald Trump's incoming administration, now the nominations process officially begins. Senate hearings are scheduled this coming week for several of Trump's picks for the Cabinet. Many have met with senators individually.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DNC chair candidates attempt to stand out at first forum
item.description: The eight people vying for the position of chair were featured last and while there was no clear winner, there was definitely a loser.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Californians contend with looting, price gouging, insurance issues
item.description: California has implemented a one-year moratorium on insurance companies canceling or refushing to renew policies for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: North Carolina's new Democratic governor pledges to rebuild after Helene, work with GOP
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina Gov. Josh Stein kicked off his term in office Saturday by praising the resilience of the state's residents following Hurricane Helene and pledging to “cut through red tape to help people rebuild quicker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SALT-focused Republicans to meet with Trump as they threaten to hold up bill
item.description: Republicans who are pledging to withhold support for President-elect Trump’s ambitious agenda if the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap is not raised are set to meet with the incoming president this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Some US lawmakers want more Christianity in the classroom. Trump could embolden their plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Conservative lawmakers across the U.S. are pushing to introduce more Christianity to public school classrooms, testing the separation of church and state by inserting Bible references into reading lessons and requiring teachers to post the Ten Commandments.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: LA Times owner: It's 'crazy' we don't elect people with competence
item.description: Los Angeles Times owner Dr. Pat Soon-Shiong said it's "crazy" his state "doesn't elect leaders with competence.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: President-elect Trump sentenced to unconditional discharge in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Homeless mental health and affordability headline NYC mayor’s State of the City address
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday laid out an ambitious agenda for 2025 while fighting for his political life. His State of the City address was the last of his first term in office as he faces a tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Sen. J.D. Vance resigns ahead of vice presidential inauguration
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (WCMH) -- U.S. Sen. J.D. Vance resigned from his seat in Congress' upper chamber on Thursday ahead of his inauguration as vice president to President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5
